$schema: See ExerciseSeedModels.cs. Fields persisted by the current schema: name, description, type, trackingType, primaryMuscle, secondaryMuscles, equipment, difficulty, mechanics (-> MovementType), estimatedCalories, averageDurationSeconds, instructions, safetyNotes (-> warnings), tags. Fields preserved for the future schema upgrade but NOT yet persisted as structured columns: slug (identity/dedup key), category & movementPattern & forceType & equipmentDetail (folded into search tags), commonMistakes, aliases. See docs/master-data/EXERCISE_SEEDING.md.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.description: Horizontal barbell press on a flat bench. A foundational upper-body strength builder for the chest, front delts, and triceps.
instructions: Lie flat, retract and depress the shoulder blades, plant the feet, keep a slight natural arch.
Unrack, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest, then press up and slightly back over the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Use a spotter or safety pins when training near maximal loads — the bar travels over the face and throat.
Keep wrists stacked over the elbows; do not bounce the bar off the chest.
commonMistakes: Flaring the elbows to 90° and stressing the shoulders.
Lifting the hips off the bench to cheat the press.

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.description: Barbell press on a 30–45° incline to bias the upper (clavicular) chest and front delts.
instructions: Set the bench to 30–45°; grip slightly wider than shoulders and unrack.
Lower to the upper chest just below the collarbone, then press up over the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Steeper inclines shift load to the shoulders — keep the angle ≤ 45°.
commonMistakes: Setting the incline too steep and turning it into an overhead press.
Letting the bar drift toward the chin.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.description: Dumbbell press on a 30–45° bench to emphasise the upper chest with a larger range of motion than the barbell.
instructions: Set the bench incline; start with the dumbbells stacked over the wrists at the bottom.
Press until the arms are extended without aggressively locking out; lower with control.
safetyNotes: Use the knee-kick to get heavy dumbbells into position and lower them to the floor under control after the set.
commonMistakes: Clashing the dumbbells together at the top and losing tension.
Bouncing out of the bottom.

exercises.name: Flat Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.description: Flat-bench dumbbell press allowing a deep stretch and independent arm work for balanced chest development.
instructions: Lie flat with dumbbells over the chest, shoulder blades retracted.
Lower to chest level with elbows ~45°, then press up and slightly together.
safetyNotes: Control the descent to the floor after the final rep to protect the shoulders.
commonMistakes: Letting the elbows flare straight out to the sides.
Using momentum instead of a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.description: Closed-chain bodyweight horizontal push. Scalable from incline (easier) to feet-elevated (harder).
instructions: Set hands slightly wider than the shoulders; brace the core so the body is a straight line.
Lower until the chest is just above the floor, then press back up keeping elbows ~45°.
safetyNotes: Regress to an incline (hands on a bench) if the hips sag or the form breaks down.
commonMistakes: Sagging or piking the hips instead of holding a rigid plank.
Only doing partial-depth reps.

exercises.name: Cable Crossover
exercises.description: Standing cable fly with constant tension through a wide adduction arc for the chest.
instructions: Set the pulleys high, take a staggered stance, and keep a soft, fixed elbow bend.
Bring the handles together in front of the chest, squeeze, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid over-stretching at the top of the cable path; keep the shoulders packed down.
commonMistakes: Turning it into a press by bending and extending the elbows.
Using too much weight and recruiting the front delts.

exercises.name: Pec Deck / Machine Fly
exercises.description: Machine isolation for horizontal shoulder adduction with a fixed, joint-friendly path.
instructions: Set the seat so the handles are at chest height; keep a slight constant elbow bend.
Bring the handles together slowly, squeeze at the midline, then return with control.
safetyNotes: Do not force the arms back past a comfortable stretch.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight closed and letting it slam back.
Shrugging the shoulders to assist.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.description: Bodyweight vertical pull with a pronated grip; a benchmark of relative upper-body pulling strength.
instructions: Hang from a pronated (overhand) grip with full elbow extension; engage the lats before pulling.
Pull the chest toward the bar, then lower under control to a full hang.
safetyNotes: Regress with band assistance or lat pulldowns if you cannot control the descent.
commonMistakes: Kipping or swinging to generate momentum.
Stopping short of a full hang at the bottom.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.description: Bodyweight vertical pull with a supinated grip, placing more emphasis on the biceps.
instructions: Hang from a supinated (underhand) shoulder-width grip with arms fully extended.
Pull until the chin clears the bar, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Use band assistance to build to a full bodyweight rep.
commonMistakes: Swinging the legs to cheat the rep.
Not reaching full extension at the bottom.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: Cable/machine vertical pull that scales easily for lat development and pull-up progressions.
instructions: Sit tall and secure the thighs; a slight lean-back is acceptable with control.
Pull the bar to the upper chest by driving the elbows down and slightly back.
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling behind the neck — it stresses the shoulders.
commonMistakes: Using body sway and momentum to move the stack.
Pulling with the arms instead of initiating from the lats.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.description: Hip-hinged horizontal barbell row for mid-back thickness and pulling strength.
instructions: Hinge until the torso is roughly 45°; brace the core and keep a neutral spine.
Row the bar toward the belly button using the back, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the spine neutral throughout — rounding under load risks the lower back.
commonMistakes: Standing up too tall and turning it into a shrug.
Jerking the bar with the lower back.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.description: Unilateral horizontal row supported on a bench, evening out left/right back strength.
instructions: Brace one hand and knee on a bench with a flat back, dumbbell hanging from the free arm.
Row the dumbbell to the hip, squeeze the back, then lower fully.
safetyNotes: Keep the spine neutral and avoid rotating the torso to heave the weight up.
commonMistakes: Twisting the torso for momentum.
Shrugging instead of driving the elbow back.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.description: Horizontal cable pull for the rhomboids, lats, and rear delts with constant tension.
instructions: Sit with a neutral spine and a slight knee bend; initiate by retracting the shoulder blades.
Row the handle to the torso without excessive swing, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding forward aggressively to chase extra range.
commonMistakes: Heaving with the lower back.
Letting the shoulders round at the end of the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dual Pulley Cable Seated Low Row
exercises.description: Seated low row using two independent cable pulleys, one per hand, for a free unilateral horizontal pull with constant tension.
instructions: Sit facing the machine and take one handle from each low pulley with a neutral grip, arms extended.
Sit tall with a slight knee bend and a neutral spine, then initiate by retracting the shoulder blades.
Row both handles to your torso, keeping the elbows close, without swinging through the lower back.
Return under control to a full stretch, then repeat for the recommended repetitions.
safetyNotes: Keep the spine neutral; avoid heaving with the lower back to move the weight.
commonMistakes: Rounding the shoulders forward at the end of the eccentric.
Using body english instead of the back muscles to row.

exercises.name: Standing Overhead Press
exercises.description: Strict standing barbell press from the shoulders to overhead; the core vertical pressing lift.
instructions: Grip just outside the shoulders, brace the core, and stack the ribcage over the pelvis.
Press the bar overhead, clearing the face with the chin, and lock out over the mid-foot.
safetyNotes: Brace hard and avoid excessive lower-back arch to press overhead.
commonMistakes: Leaning back into a standing incline press.
Failing to move the head 'through' at lockout.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Supported vertical dumbbell press for the deltoids with an independent range of motion.
instructions: Sit with back support, dumbbells at shoulder height, wrists stacked over the elbows.
Press overhead until nearly locked out, then lower to ear level under control.
safetyNotes: Use a supported seat for heavy sets to protect the lower back.
commonMistakes: Clashing the dumbbells overhead.
Bouncing out of the bottom with momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Isolation for the lateral (medial) deltoid, building shoulder width.
instructions: Stand tall with a slight, fixed bend in the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells out to shoulder height leading with the elbows, then lower slowly.
safetyNotes: Use light weight — momentum here shifts load to the traps and neck.
commonMistakes: Swinging the weights up with the whole body.
Shrugging the traps at the top.

exercises.name: Face Pull (Rope)
exercises.description: Cable external-rotation pull to the face for rear delts and rotator-cuff/shoulder health.
instructions: Set the rope at face height; pull toward the forehead while splitting the rope apart.
Pause with the upper arms horizontal and the knuckles facing back, then return slowly.
safetyNotes: Keep the weight light and the movement smooth to protect the shoulder.
commonMistakes: Using too much weight and rowing instead of externally rotating.
Letting the elbows drop below the shoulders.

exercises.name: Rear-Delt Dumbbell Fly
exercises.description: Bent-over reverse fly isolating the posterior deltoids for balanced shoulders and posture.
instructions: Hinge forward with a flat back and a slight, fixed elbow bend.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides squeezing the rear delts, then lower slowly.
safetyNotes: Support the chest on an incline bench if the lower back fatigues.
commonMistakes: Using momentum and shrugging.
Turning it into an upright row by leading with the hands.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.description: Resistance-band horizontal pull for upper-back activation and warm-up.
instructions: Hold a band at shoulder width with arms straight out in front.
Pull the band apart to chest height by squeezing the shoulder blades, then return slowly.
safetyNotes: Choose a band tension you can control through a full, smooth range.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows to cheat the movement.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.description: Classic standing biceps isolation with a straight or EZ barbell.
instructions: Stand with elbows tucked under the shoulders and a shoulder-width grip.
Curl the bar up by flexing the biceps, then lower under control without swinging.
safetyNotes: Avoid hyperextending the lower back to heave heavy curls.
commonMistakes: Swinging the hips and torso to move the bar.
Letting the elbows drift forward at the top.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.description: Alternating or simultaneous dumbbell curl with optional supination for the biceps.
instructions: Stand or sit tall with dumbbells at the sides, palms facing in.
Curl up while rotating the palm to face up, squeeze, then lower with control.
safetyNotes: Keep the upper arm still — only the forearm should move.
commonMistakes: Swinging the dumbbells with body english.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.description: Neutral-grip dumbbell curl emphasising the brachialis and brachioradialis (forearm).
instructions: Hold the dumbbells with a neutral (palms-facing) grip, elbows tucked.
Curl up keeping the neutral grip, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the wrists neutral and the elbows pinned.
commonMistakes: Rotating the wrists into a standard curl.
Using momentum from the shoulders.

exercises.name: Triceps Rope Pushdown
exercises.description: Cable extension emphasising the triceps, with a rope allowing a strong end-range squeeze.
instructions: Set a rope at the top of a cable stack; pin the elbows to the ribs.
Extend the arms fully and spread the rope at the bottom, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows still — let only the forearms move.
commonMistakes: Letting the elbows flare and drift forward.
Leaning over the rope to push with bodyweight.

exercises.name: Overhead Dumbbell Triceps Extension
exercises.description: Seated or standing overhead extension that biases the long head of the triceps via a deep stretch.
instructions: Hold one dumbbell overhead with both hands, elbows pointing forward.
Lower the weight behind the head, then extend back to lockout keeping the elbows in.
safetyNotes: Control the stretch at the bottom; do not let the elbows flare wide.
commonMistakes: Flaring the elbows out to the sides.
Using the shoulders to press the weight up.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
exercises.description: Shoulder-width barbell bench press that shifts emphasis to the triceps.
instructions: Grip the bar about shoulder-width; keep the elbows tucked close to the body.
Lower to the lower chest, then press up keeping the elbows in.
safetyNotes: Do not grip so narrow that the wrists are uncomfortable; use a spotter when heavy.
commonMistakes: Gripping too narrow and straining the wrists.
Flaring the elbows and losing the triceps emphasis.

exercises.name: Bench Dip
exercises.description: Bodyweight triceps extension off a bench; scalable by foot position.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench, hands beside the hips, and slide the hips off the edge.
Lower by bending the elbows to ~90°, then press back up.
safetyNotes: Avoid descending too deep, which over-stresses the front of the shoulder.
commonMistakes: Letting the shoulders shrug up toward the ears.
Going too deep and straining the shoulder.

exercises.name: Back Squat (High-Bar)
exercises.description: Barbell squat with the bar on the upper traps; the foundational lower-body strength lift.
instructions: Brace the core and break at the hips and knees together, keeping the torso upright.
Descend to at least parallel if mobility allows, then drive up evenly through the mid-foot.
safetyNotes: Squat inside a rack with safety pins set; keep a neutral spine under load.
commonMistakes: Knees caving inward on the way up.
Rounding the lower back at the bottom ('butt wink').

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.description: Front-racked barbell squat that demands an upright torso and biases the quads.
instructions: Rack the bar on the front delts with high elbows; brace the core.
Squat down keeping the torso vertical and elbows up, then drive back to standing.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows high — dropping them spills the bar forward.
commonMistakes: Letting the elbows drop and the chest cave.
Rising hips-first and folding forward.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: Machine squat-pattern press with a fixed path and high load potential for the quads.
instructions: Set the feet mid-platform at shoulder width; unlock the sled with control.
Lower until a comfortable depth without the lower back rounding off the pad, then press up.
safetyNotes: Never lock out and bounce; keep a slight knee bend at the top.
commonMistakes: Lowering so deep the hips curl off the seat.
Locking the knees aggressively at the top.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.description: Front-loaded squat holding a single dumbbell or kettlebell; excellent for grooving squat depth.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell at the chest with both hands; stand a touch wider than shoulder width.
Sit between the hips keeping the heels down and elbows inside the knees, then stand.
safetyNotes: Keep the chest tall; the weight at the chest helps counterbalance.
commonMistakes: Heels rising off the floor.
Rounding forward as depth increases.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.description: Open-chain machine isolation for the quadriceps.
instructions: Set the pad on the lower shins and align the knees with the machine pivot.
Extend the knees to straight, squeeze the quads, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid snapping into full lockout with heavy load if the knees are sensitive.
commonMistakes: Using momentum to kick the weight up.
Letting the weight stack slam down on the eccentric.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.description: Alternating forward lunge for single-leg strength, balance, and quad/glute development.
instructions: Step forward far enough that the front knee tracks over the ankle; keep a tall torso.
Touch the back knee softly, then push through the front heel to step into the next lunge.
safetyNotes: Add load (dumbbells) only once balance and form are consistent.
commonMistakes: Taking too short a step so the knee shoots past the toes.
Leaning the torso forward over the front leg.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.description: Rear-foot-elevated split squat; a demanding unilateral builder for quads and glutes.
instructions: Elevate the rear foot on a bench; set the front foot far enough forward for a vertical shin.
Lower until the front thigh is parallel, then drive up through the front heel.
safetyNotes: Master the bodyweight version and balance before adding dumbbells.
commonMistakes: Front foot too close, pushing the knee well past the toes.
Putting weight on the back foot instead of the front leg.

exercises.name: Wall Sit (Timed Hold)
exercises.description: Isometric quad hold against a wall — logged purely by how long you hold it.
instructions: Slide down a wall until the thighs are parallel to the floor with knees over ankles.
Brace and hold for time, breathing steadily.
safetyNotes: Stop if the knees feel pinched; raise the hips slightly to reduce knee angle.
commonMistakes: Sitting too low so the knees pass far over the toes.
Holding the breath instead of breathing steadily.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description: Hip-hinge with soft knees that biases the hamstrings and glutes through a loaded stretch.
instructions: Push the hips back to slide the bar down the legs, keeping the spine neutral.
Feel a hamstring stretch around mid-shin, then drive the hips forward to stand tall.
safetyNotes: Keep the bar close to the body and never round the lower back.
commonMistakes: Turning it into a squat by bending the knees too much.
Rounding the back to reach lower.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.description: Full-body hip-hinge pulling a barbell from the floor; a primary posterior-chain strength lift.
instructions: Set the bar over the mid-foot; hinge to grip just outside the knees with a braced, neutral spine.
Drive the floor away, keep the bar close, and stand tall without hyperextending the lower back.
safetyNotes: Brace hard and keep the spine neutral; reset each rep rather than bouncing.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back to start the pull.
Letting the bar drift away from the shins.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.description: Machine isolation flexing the knee against resistance to target the hamstrings.
instructions: Lie face down with the pad on the lower calves and the knees just off the bench edge.
Curl the heels toward the glutes, squeeze, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting the hips off the pad to heave the weight.
commonMistakes: Jerking the hips up to assist the curl.
Using a partial range of motion.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Bench-supported barbell hip extension; a premier glute strength and hypertrophy builder.
instructions: Set the upper back on a bench with the bar over the hips (use a pad); feet flat, shins vertical at the top.
Drive through the heels to full hip extension, squeeze the glutes, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the chin tucked and ribs down; do not hyperextend the lower back at lockout.
commonMistakes: Overextending the lumbar spine instead of the hips at the top.
Pushing through the toes rather than the heels.

exercises.name: Seated Machine Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Seated machine hip extension with the resistance pad across the lap; a stable, beginner-friendly glute builder on a fixed path.
instructions: Sit upright in the machine with your back against the pad and the lap pad secured across your hips.
Set your feet flat and shoulder-width with your shins roughly vertical at the top of the movement.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips to push the pad forward, squeezing the glutes.
Pause briefly at full extension, then return under control without letting the weight stack rest.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
safetyNotes: Extend through the hips, not the lower back; keep the ribs down and chin tucked at lockout.
commonMistakes: Pushing through the toes instead of the heels.
Arching the lumbar spine to chase extra range.

exercises.name: Perched Machine Hip Thrust
exercises.description: Hip thrust performed on a perched glute machine: the torso is supported forward with a pad over the hips while driving hip extension against a loaded lever.
instructions: Step into the perched glute machine and position the pad across your hips with your torso supported on the forward rest.
Set your feet on the platform about shoulder-width with your shins vertical at the top.
Brace your core, then drive through your heels to extend the hips against the pad, squeezing the glutes.
Hold the contraction briefly at full extension, then lower under control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
safetyNotes: Keep the movement at the hips; avoid hyperextending the lower back at the top.
commonMistakes: Cutting the range short and not reaching full hip extension.
Driving through the toes rather than the heels.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Floor-based bodyweight hip extension; a beginner-friendly glute activator.
instructions: Lie on the back with knees bent and feet flat, heels close to the hips.
Drive through the heels to lift the hips, squeeze the glutes at the top, then lower.
safetyNotes: Posterior-tilt the pelvis slightly so the work comes from the glutes, not the lower back.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back instead of squeezing the glutes.
Letting the knees cave inward.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.description: Standing plantarflexion (machine or dumbbell) targeting the gastrocnemius.
instructions: Place the balls of the feet on the platform with the heels free; stand tall.
Rise onto the toes through a full range, pause, then lower the heels for a deep stretch.
safetyNotes: Control the bottom stretch; do not bounce out of it.
commonMistakes: Using a tiny, bouncy range of motion.
Bending the knees to cheat the raise.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.description: Bent-knee plantarflexion that biases the soleus.
instructions: Sit with the balls of the feet on the platform and the pad on the lower thighs.
Raise the heels as high as possible, pause, then lower into a full stretch.
safetyNotes: Keep the movement slow and controlled through the full range.
commonMistakes: Short, fast partial reps.
Skipping the bottom stretch.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Anti-extension core brace in a prone straight line — logged by how long you hold it.
instructions: Set the forearms (or hands) under the shoulders; form a straight line from head to heels.
Brace the abs and glutes and hold — no sag or pike — breathing steadily.
safetyNotes: Stop the set when the hips start to sag rather than grinding out a broken hold.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips sag or pike up.
Holding the breath.

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.description: Hanging hip flexion for the lower abdominals and hip flexors.
instructions: Hang from a bar with minimal swing and the shoulders engaged.
Flex the hips to bring the knees toward the chest, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging — control both directions; progress to straight-leg raises.
commonMistakes: Using a big kip/swing to throw the knees up.
Only raising the knees a few inches.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.description: Kneeling cable spinal flexion that allows progressive loading of the abs.
instructions: Kneel below a high cable holding a rope at the head/neck; hinge slightly at the hips.
Crunch by flexing the spine and bringing the elbows toward the thighs, then return slowly.
safetyNotes: Flex the spine to crunch — do not just bow at the hips with a flat back.
commonMistakes: Pulling with the arms instead of crunching the abs.
Turning it into a hip hinge with a rigid spine.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: Seated rotational core exercise for the obliques (optionally weighted).
instructions: Sit with knees bent and torso leaned back ~45°, bracing the core.
Rotate the torso to tap the hands (or a weight) side to side under control.
safetyNotes: Rotate from the trunk, not by yanking the arms; keep the spine long.
commonMistakes: Moving only the arms while the torso stays still.
Rounding and collapsing the lower back.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Supine anti-extension core drill coordinating opposite arm and leg; very lower-back friendly.
instructions: Lie on the back with arms reaching to the ceiling and hips/knees bent to 90°.
Slowly lower the opposite arm and leg while pressing the lower back into the floor, then switch.
safetyNotes: Keep the lower back flat against the floor throughout.
commonMistakes: Letting the lower back arch off the floor.
Moving too fast and losing the brace.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description: Explosive two-handed hip-hinge swing for posterior-chain power and conditioning.
instructions: Hinge and hike the kettlebell back between the legs, keeping a neutral spine.
Snap the hips forward to float the bell to chest height — power comes from the hips, not the arms.
safetyNotes: It is a hip hinge, not a squat or a front raise; keep the back neutral and let the hips drive.
commonMistakes: Squatting the bell up instead of hinging.
Lifting with the arms/shoulders rather than the hips.

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry
exercises.description: Loaded carry for grip, core stability, and posture — log it by distance, time, or load.
instructions: Pick up heavy dumbbells at the sides; pack the shoulders down and brace the core.
Walk with short, controlled steps and tall posture, breathing behind the brace.
safetyNotes: Set the weights down with a hinge, not a rounded back, at the end of the carry.
commonMistakes: Leaning to one side under uneven load.
Shuffling with a rounded upper back.

exercises.name: Sled Push
exercises.description: Loaded sled push for lower-body power and conditioning — flexible logging by distance, time, or rounds.
instructions: Grip the sled posts with a low body angle and arms extended; brace the core.
Drive through the balls of the feet with powerful steps for the prescribed distance or time.
safetyNotes: Keep the spine neutral; let the legs do the work, not the lower back.
commonMistakes: Standing too upright and losing drive.
Taking tiny steps instead of powerful strides.

exercises.name: Burpee HIIT Circuit
exercises.description: High-intensity interval circuit of burpees — log the number of rounds and the work duration per round.
instructions: From standing, drop to a plank, lower the chest to the floor, then explode back up into a jump.
Work the interval, rest, and repeat for the prescribed number of rounds.
safetyNotes: Scale the depth and the jump if form degrades as fatigue accumulates.
commonMistakes: Letting the lower back sag in the plank/push-up phase.
Going so fast that landings become uncontrolled.

exercises.name: Assault Bike / Air Bike
exercises.description: Full-body conditioning on an air-resistance bike — log by duration, distance, calories, or heart rate.
instructions: Drive the arms and legs together for a smooth, continuous rhythm.
Adjust the pace for steady-state work or hard intervals.
safetyNotes: Ease into the first minute; air resistance scales hard with effort.
commonMistakes: Going out too hot and fading during intervals.
Only pushing with the legs and ignoring the arms.

exercises.name: Treadmill Run (Easy)
exercises.description: Steady-state running on a treadmill for aerobic base building.
instructions: Warm up, then settle into a conversational pace landing mid-foot with a short stride.
Keep posture tall and breathe rhythmically; cool down at the end.
safetyNotes: Clip on the safety stop key in case of a stumble.
commonMistakes: Overstriding and heel-striking hard.
Holding the handrails, which alters running mechanics.

exercises.name: Rowing Machine (Erg)
exercises.description: Full-body indoor rowing — log by distance, duration, calories, or heart rate.
instructions: Drive in order: legs, then hips, then arms; reverse the order on the recovery.
Keep a steady stroke rate and a smooth, connected pull.
safetyNotes: Avoid yanking with the lower back at the catch; lead with the legs.
commonMistakes: Breaking the arms early before the legs finish driving.
Rounding the back at the catch.

exercises.name: Jump Rope
exercises.description: Skipping for cardiovascular conditioning, footwork, and coordination — interval or steady.
instructions: Turn the rope with the wrists, not the arms; stay light on the balls of the feet.
Keep small, quick hops and an upright posture; work in timed rounds.
safetyNotes: Skip on a forgiving surface and wear supportive shoes to spare the calves and feet.
commonMistakes: Jumping too high and landing heavily.
Swinging from the shoulders instead of the wrists.

exercises.name: Thoracic Extension on Foam Roller
exercises.description: Mobility drill restoring upper-back extension over a foam roller.
instructions: Place the roller across the mid-thoracic spine; support the head with the hands.
Extend back over the roller in small segments without arching from the lower back.
safetyNotes: Extend from the upper back only; keep the ribs from flaring and the lower back from over-arching.
commonMistakes: Hinging from the lower back instead of the thoracic spine.
Rolling the neck or lumbar region.

exercises.name: Pigeon Stretch (Static)
exercises.description: Static hip external-rotation stretch for the glutes and hip capsule.
instructions: Bring one shin in front with the hip externally rotated; extend the other leg back.
Square the hips and breathe into the stretch for 30–60 seconds per side.
safetyNotes: Ease off if there is any sharp knee pain; keep the front shin angle comfortable.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips rotate open instead of staying square.
Forcing the front shin to 90° before the hip is ready.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Half-kneeling or standing lunge stretch for the hip flexors, useful after long sitting.
instructions: Adopt a split stance (or half-kneel); tuck the pelvis under to flatten the lower back.
Shift the hips gently forward until a stretch is felt at the front of the rear hip; hold and switch.
safetyNotes: Drive the stretch with a posterior pelvic tilt rather than arching the lower back.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back instead of tucking the pelvis.
Pushing the front knee far past the toes.

exercises.name: Incline Smith Press
exercises.description: Incline pressing on a Smith machine; the fixed bar path biases the upper chest while reducing stabiliser demand.
instructions: Set an incline bench under the Smith bar; unrack and lower the bar to the upper chest.
Press up and slightly back along the fixed path, stopping short of a hard lockout.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulder blades retracted; the fixed path punishes poor bench positioning.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.description: Seated horizontal press on a selectorised or plate-loaded chest-press machine.
instructions: Set the seat so the handles align with mid-chest; press out without locking the elbows hard.
Control the return until a light stretch is felt across the chest.
safetyNotes: Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears during the press.

exercises.name: Shoulder Press Machine
exercises.description: Seated vertical press on a shoulder-press machine targeting the front and side delts.
instructions: Set the seat so the handles sit at shoulder height; press overhead without locking out hard.
Lower under control until the elbows are level with the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Keep the lower back against the pad; do not arch to push heavier loads.

exercises.name: Lying Cuff Lateral
exercises.description: Side-lying dumbbell external rotation that targets the rotator cuff (infraspinatus / teres minor).
instructions: Lie on your side with the working arm on top, elbow pinned to the ribs at 90 degrees.
Rotate the forearm up toward the ceiling, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Use a light weight; the cuff fatigues quickly and is easy to strain.

exercises.name: Cuff Pushdown
exercises.description: Cable rotator-cuff pushdown / external-rotation strengthener for the posterior shoulder.
instructions: Set a cable to mid height; keep the elbow tucked and rotate the forearm down and out.
Return slowly, resisting the cable through the full range.
safetyNotes: Keep the movement strict and light; avoid recruiting the lats or triceps.

exercises.name: Shoulder Stretch
exercises.description: Static cross-body and overhead shoulder stretch for the deltoids and capsule.
instructions: Draw one arm across the body and gently press it with the opposite hand; hold.
Switch sides and repeat, breathing steadily throughout.
safetyNotes: Ease into the stretch; never force a painful end range.

exercises.name: Triceps Stretch
exercises.description: Static overhead triceps stretch.
instructions: Raise one arm overhead and bend the elbow so the hand drops behind the neck.
Gently press the elbow back with the other hand; hold, then switch.
safetyNotes: Keep the neck neutral; do not push the head forward.

exercises.name: Upper Back Row
exercises.description: Machine/cable row angled to bias the upper back, rhomboids and rear delts.
instructions: Set a high-to-mid pull line; row the handles toward the upper chest, squeezing the shoulder blades.
Return under control without letting the shoulders round forward.
safetyNotes: Drive with the back, not by leaning the torso back excessively.

exercises.name: Barbell Shrug
exercises.description: Barbell shrug targeting the upper trapezius.
instructions: Hold a barbell at arm's length; elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid rolling the shoulders; shrug straight up and down.

exercises.name: Incline Cable Curl
exercises.description: Cable biceps curl performed from an incline bench for a stretched starting position.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a low cable; let the arm hang and curl with the elbow fixed.
Squeeze at the top, then lower until the biceps is fully stretched.
safetyNotes: Keep the upper arm still; do not let the elbow drift forward.

exercises.name: Standing Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Single-leg standing leg-curl machine isolating the hamstrings.
instructions: Brace against the pad and curl the heel toward the glute one leg at a time.
Lower under control to a full stretch before the next rep.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips pressed to the pad; avoid swinging the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description: Hip-hinge with dumbbells emphasising the hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Hold dumbbells in front of the thighs; hinge at the hips pushing them back, knees soft.
Lower until a hamstring stretch is felt, then drive the hips forward to stand.
safetyNotes: Keep the back flat and the dumbbells close to the legs throughout.

exercises.name: Back Extension
exercises.description: Hyperextension-bench back raise for the glutes, hamstrings and spinal erectors.
instructions: Anchor the thighs on the pad; hinge at the hips to lower the torso, then raise to a straight line.
Avoid hyperextending past neutral at the top.
safetyNotes: Do not round or over-arch the lower back at the top of the rep.

exercises.name: Frog Pump
exercises.description: Glute-focused bridge with the soles of the feet together and knees splayed.
instructions: Lie on your back, press the soles of the feet together with knees out.
Drive the hips up by squeezing the glutes, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the rib cage down; extend through the hips, not the lower back.

exercises.name: Assisted Chin-Up
exercises.description: Counter-weighted assisted chin-up machine for the lats and biceps.
instructions: Kneel on the assist pad with a supinated grip; pull the chest toward the bar.
Lower under control to a full hang each rep.
safetyNotes: Set enough assistance to keep the reps strict and controlled.

exercises.name: Lat Stretch
exercises.description: Static overhead/side lat stretch.
instructions: Grip a rack at chest height, hinge back and sink the hips to lengthen one side.
Hold, breathe into the stretch, then switch sides.
safetyNotes: Keep a light grip and avoid yanking the shoulder.

exercises.name: Decline Machine Press
exercises.description: Plate-loaded / hammer-style decline press biasing the lower chest.
instructions: Set the seat so the handles align with the lower chest; press out and slightly down.
Return under control to a comfortable stretch.
safetyNotes: Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows through the press.

exercises.name: Machine Dip
exercises.description: Seated assisted/loaded dip machine for the triceps and lower chest.
instructions: Grip the handles and press down until the arms are nearly straight.
Return under control until the elbows reach about 90 degrees.
safetyNotes: Keep the shoulders down and back; avoid collapsing forward.

exercises.name: Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Static doorway/wall pec stretch.
instructions: Place the forearm on a doorframe or wall at shoulder height; rotate the torso away.
Hold the stretch across the chest, then switch sides.
safetyNotes: Avoid forcing the shoulder into an end range that pinches.

exercises.name: Incline Triceps Extension
exercises.description: Dumbbell triceps extension on an incline bench for a stretched long head.
instructions: Lie back on an incline bench; lower the dumbbells beside the head, elbows fixed.
Extend the elbows to lockout, keeping the upper arms still.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows from flaring wide to protect the joint.

exercises.name: Crunch
exercises.description: Floor abdominal crunch flexing the upper spine.
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent; curl the rib cage toward the pelvis.
Lower under control without fully relaxing the abs.
safetyNotes: Avoid yanking on the neck; lead with the rib cage.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.description: Hanging raise from a bar targeting the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Hang from a bar; raise the legs in front of you, curling the pelvis up.
Lower under control without swinging.
safetyNotes: Control the descent to avoid swinging and lower-back strain.

exercises.name: Captain's Chair Leg Raise
exercises.description: Lower-ab leg/knee raise performed on a captain's-chair / dip station.
instructions: Support yourself on the forearm pads; raise the knees or legs toward the chest.
Lower under control without arching the lower back.
safetyNotes: Keep the back against the pad and avoid swinging.

exercises.name: V-Bar Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: Close neutral-grip (V-bar) lat pulldown emphasising the mid-back and lats.
instructions: Attach a V-bar to the pulldown; pull it to the upper chest, driving the elbows down.
Return under control to a full stretch overhead.
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning back excessively or using momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Neutral-Grip Chest Press
exercises.description: Flat dumbbell press with palms facing each other to reduce shoulder stress.
instructions: Lie flat holding dumbbells with a neutral (palms-in) grip over the chest.
Lower to the sides of the chest, then press back up.
safetyNotes: Keep the wrists stacked and lower the dumbbells under control.

exercises.name: Dips
exercises.description: Bodyweight parallel-bar dips for the triceps and lower chest.
instructions: Support yourself on parallel bars; lower until the elbows reach about 90 degrees.
Press back up to a stable lockout.
safetyNotes: Avoid descending too deep if it strains the shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
exercises.description: Single-dumbbell pullover working the chest and lats through a large arc.
instructions: Lie across or along a bench holding one dumbbell over the chest.
Lower it behind the head in an arc, then pull it back over the chest.
safetyNotes: Keep a slight elbow bend and avoid over-stretching the shoulders.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Fly
exercises.description: Incline-bench dumbbell fly isolating the upper chest.
instructions: On an incline bench, open the arms in a wide arc with a soft elbow bend.
Bring the dumbbells together over the chest, squeezing the pecs.
safetyNotes: Do not let the dumbbells drop too low and strain the shoulders.

exercises.name: Hip Abductor (Machine)
exercises.description: Seated hip-abductor machine targeting the gluteus medius and minimus.
instructions: Sit with the pads against the outer thighs; press the knees apart.
Return under control, resisting the stack.
safetyNotes: Keep the torso still; let the hips do the work.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.description: Biceps curl performed on a preacher bench so the upper arms are fixed against the pad, removing momentum and biasing the lower biceps through a strong stretch.
instructions: Seat the back of the upper arms flat on the preacher pad with a shoulder-width underhand grip.
Curl the bar up by flexing the biceps, then lower slowly until the elbows are nearly straight.
safetyNotes: Do not bounce out of the bottom — the stretched position is where the biceps tendon is most vulnerable.
commonMistakes: Lifting the elbows off the pad to cheat the rep.
Dropping the weight at the bottom instead of lowering under control.

exercises.name: Hip Adductor (Machine)
exercises.description: Seated hip-adductor machine targeting the inner-thigh adductors by squeezing the knees together against the stack.
instructions: Sit with the pads against the inner thighs and the legs spread to a comfortable stretch.
Squeeze the knees together against the resistance, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Set the starting width to a comfortable stretch; do not force an aggressive range under load.

exercises.name: Standing Cable Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.description: Standing overhead cable extension with a rope, keeping constant tension on the long head of the triceps through a deep stretch.
instructions: Set a rope low (or face away from a high pulley) and step forward so the cable runs behind the head with the elbows high and tucked.
Extend the arms fully overhead, then lower under control until the triceps is deeply stretched.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows pointing forward and still — let only the forearms move to protect the elbow joint.
commonMistakes: Letting the elbows flare wide and drift down.
Using bodyweight to lean and push instead of extending the elbows.

exercises.name: High Cable Rear Delt Fly (Cuffs)
exercises.description: Bilateral high-cable reverse fly using wrist cuffs, pulling out and back to isolate the posterior deltoids with constant cable tension.
instructions: Attach a cuff to each high pulley, cross the cables and grip neither handle — drive from the wrists.
Pull the cuffs out and back in a wide arc squeezing the rear delts, then return slowly without letting the shoulders roll forward.
safetyNotes: Keep a slight fixed elbow bend; do not turn it into a straight-arm pulldown.
commonMistakes: Using too much weight and recruiting the lats and traps.
Shrugging the shoulders up instead of driving the elbows back.

exercises.name: Stationary Bike
exercises.description: Steady-state upright cycling on a stationary bike for low-impact aerobic conditioning.
instructions: Set the saddle so the knee is slightly bent at the bottom of the stroke.
Hold a steady cadence and resistance for the target duration.
safetyNotes: Keep the resistance high enough that you are not bouncing in the saddle.

exercises.name: Recumbent Bike
exercises.description: Seated reclined cycling that supports the back — gentle low-impact cardio.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the legs almost fully extend at the far pedal.
Pedal at a steady cadence for the target duration.
safetyNotes: Keep the back supported against the seat throughout.

exercises.name: Elliptical Trainer
exercises.description: Low-impact full-stride cardio driving the pedals and handles together.
instructions: Stand tall, drive through the legs and push/pull the handles in rhythm.
Hold a steady stride and resistance for the target duration.
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning on the handles — keep the weight through the legs.

exercises.name: Stair Climber (StepMill)
exercises.description: Continuous stair-stepping cardio that heavily taxes the legs and glutes.
instructions: Stand tall and step in a controlled rhythm without gripping the rails for support.
Hold a steady pace for the target duration.
safetyNotes: Do not hunch over or hang on the handrails — it removes the training effect.

exercises.name: Incline Treadmill Walk
exercises.description: Brisk uphill treadmill walking — high-calorie, joint-friendly steady cardio.
instructions: Set a moderate speed and a 6–12% incline; walk tall without holding the rails.
Maintain the pace and incline for the target duration.
safetyNotes: Let go of the handrails so the incline actually loads the legs.

exercises.name: Outdoor Run
exercises.description: Continuous outdoor running over ground for aerobic conditioning.
instructions: Run at a conversational to moderate pace with a relaxed upright posture.
Hold the effort for the target distance or duration.
safetyNotes: Build mileage gradually to protect the knees, shins and ankles.

exercises.name: Outdoor Cycling
exercises.description: Riding a bicycle outdoors for endurance and aerobic conditioning.
instructions: Maintain a smooth cadence over varied terrain in an appropriate gear.
Ride for the target distance or duration.
safetyNotes: Wear a helmet and stay aware of traffic.

exercises.name: Walking
exercises.description: Steady walking for easy, accessible low-intensity cardio and recovery.
instructions: Walk at a brisk, comfortable pace with a tall posture and natural arm swing.
Continue for the target steps, distance or duration.
safetyNotes: Wear supportive footwear for longer walks.

exercises.name: Swimming
exercises.description: Full-body, zero-impact aerobic conditioning in the water.
instructions: Swim continuous laps in your chosen stroke with steady breathing.
Hold the effort for the target distance or duration.
safetyNotes: Only swim where supervised or with a buddy.

exercises.name: Ski Erg
exercises.description: Standing double-pole machine that mimics cross-country skiing — upper-body-led cardio.
instructions: Reach tall, then drive the handles down and back by hinging and pulling through the lats and triceps.
Return to full extension and repeat at a steady pace.
safetyNotes: Hinge from the hips and keep a braced, neutral spine.

exercises.name: Rowing Intervals
exercises.description: High-intensity interval rowing alternating hard pulls with recovery.
instructions: Row hard for the work interval driving with the legs then back then arms.
Row easy or rest for the recovery interval; repeat for the set.
safetyNotes: Lead each stroke with the legs, not the lower back.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Fast alternating knee drives from a plank — a bodyweight cardio and core burner.
instructions: Start in a high plank with a braced core and flat back.
Drive the knees toward the chest one at a time as fast as control allows.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips low and level — do not let them pike up.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.description: Running in place driving the knees up high — a simple HIIT and warm-up drill.
instructions: Run in place driving each knee up to hip height with a quick arm swing.
Stay on the balls of the feet and keep the pace fast.
safetyNotes: Land softly through the forefoot to spare the knees.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Classic full-body jumping calisthenic for warm-ups and conditioning.
instructions: Jump the feet out while raising the arms overhead, then jump back together.
Keep a steady rhythm for the work interval.
safetyNotes: Land softly with slightly bent knees.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.description: Explosive two-foot jump onto a raised box to build lower-body power.
instructions: Dip into a quarter squat and swing the arms, then jump and land softly on top of the box.
Stand tall on the box, then step (do not jump) back down and reset.
safetyNotes: Always step down rather than jumping down to protect the Achilles and knees.

exercises.name: Battle Ropes
exercises.description: Rhythmic heavy-rope waves for upper-body conditioning and metabolic work.
instructions: Hold a rope end in each hand in an athletic stance with a braced core.
Drive alternating or double waves down the ropes for the work interval.
safetyNotes: Keep the knees soft and the spine neutral; power the waves from the whole body.

exercises.name: Shadow Boxing
exercises.description: Continuous punching and footwork against an imaginary opponent for cardio.
instructions: Move on the balls of the feet throwing combinations of jabs, crosses and hooks.
Keep the hands up and stay light for the work interval.
safetyNotes: Do not fully lock out the elbows when punching.

exercises.name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.description: Barbell press on a decline bench to bias the lower (sternal) chest.
instructions: Set a 15–30° decline and lock the feet; unrack with a slightly wider than shoulder grip.
Lower the bar to the lower chest, then press up over the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Use a spotter — bar removal and re-racking are awkward on a decline.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.description: Close-hands push-up forming a diamond shape to emphasise the triceps and inner chest.
instructions: Place the hands together under the chest so the thumbs and index fingers form a diamond.
Lower with the elbows tucked, then press back to lockout.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows close to the body to protect the shoulders.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.description: Chest-supported or landmine barbell row that loads the mid-back with a strong squeeze.
instructions: Straddle the bar, hinge to ~45° with a flat back and grip the handle.
Row the weight to the lower chest squeezing the shoulder blades, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the spine neutral; do not jerk the weight up with the lower back.

exercises.name: Pendlay Row
exercises.description: Explosive barbell row from a dead stop on the floor each rep for mid-back power.
instructions: Hinge to a flat, near-parallel torso and grip the bar over the mid-foot.
Row explosively to the lower chest, then return the bar fully to the floor between reps.
safetyNotes: Keep the torso fixed and parallel; let the back rest only between reps, not mid-set.

exercises.name: Inverted Row
exercises.description: Horizontal bodyweight row under a fixed bar — a scalable pull for the whole back.
instructions: Hang under a waist-high bar with a straight body and an overhand grip.
Pull the chest to the bar squeezing the blades, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips up so the body stays in a straight line.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.description: Cable pullover-style lat isolation with the elbows nearly locked.
instructions: Stand tall at a high pulley holding a straight bar with nearly straight arms.
Drive the bar down to the thighs in an arc using the lats, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Keep a small fixed elbow bend; do not turn it into a triceps pushdown.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.description: Dumbbell shrug for direct upper-trap development with a long range.
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides.
Shrug the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause, then lower fully.
safetyNotes: Do not roll the shoulders — shrug straight up and down.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
exercises.description: Wide-stance deadlift with a more upright torso, sharing load across legs, glutes and back.
instructions: Take a wide stance with toes out, grip inside the knees, hips down and chest up.
Drive the floor away and stand tall, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the bar against the body and the spine neutral throughout.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.description: Hex/trap-bar deadlift with neutral handles — a knee-friendly, easy-to-learn pull.
instructions: Stand inside the trap bar, grip the neutral handles, hips back and chest up.
Drive through the floor to stand tall, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Brace the core and keep the back neutral on every rep.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.description: Passive hang from a pull-up bar for grip endurance, shoulder health and decompression.
instructions: Grip a pull-up bar slightly wider than the shoulders and hang with the arms straight.
Stay relaxed and breathe for the target time.
safetyNotes: Build hang time gradually to avoid grip and elbow strain.

exercises.name: Arnold Press
exercises.description: Rotating seated dumbbell press that hits all three deltoid heads through a long arc.
instructions: Start seated with the dumbbells in front at shoulder height, palms facing you.
Press up while rotating the palms to face forward, then reverse on the way down.
safetyNotes: Keep the core braced and avoid arching the lower back.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Single-arm cable side raise giving the side delt constant tension through the range.
instructions: Stand side-on to a low pulley and hold the handle across the body.
Raise the arm out to shoulder height with a fixed slight elbow bend, then lower slowly.
safetyNotes: Lead with the elbow, not the hand, and avoid shrugging.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.description: Dumbbell raise to the front isolating the anterior deltoid.
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells in front of the thighs.
Raise one or both arms to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend, then lower slowly.
safetyNotes: Do not swing or lean back to throw the weight up.

exercises.name: Upright Row
exercises.description: Vertical pull of a barbell or cable up the front of the body for delts and traps.
instructions: Hold the bar at thigh width with an overhand grip.
Pull the bar up the body leading with the elbows to about chest height, then lower.
safetyNotes: Stop at chest height and avoid a narrow grip if the shoulders pinch.

exercises.name: Reverse Pec Deck
exercises.description: Seated machine reverse fly isolating the rear delts with a fixed path.
instructions: Sit facing the pad and grip the handles with arms extended in front.
Sweep the arms out and back squeezing the rear delts, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Keep a soft fixed elbow and avoid shrugging the traps.

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.description: Standing biceps curl on a low cable for constant tension top to bottom.
instructions: Stand tall at a low pulley with a straight bar and elbows pinned to the sides.
Curl the bar up by flexing the biceps, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the elbows still; do not let them drift forward.

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
exercises.description: Seated single-arm curl braced against the inner thigh for peak biceps isolation.
instructions: Sit and brace the working elbow against the inner thigh, arm hanging straight.
Curl the dumbbell to the shoulder squeezing hard, then lower fully.
safetyNotes: Keep the upper arm fixed against the thigh — no swinging.

exercises.name: Lying Triceps Extension (Skullcrusher)
exercises.description: Lying EZ-bar extension lowering to the forehead to load all three triceps heads.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding an EZ bar over the chest with arms vertical.
Bend only the elbows to lower the bar toward the forehead, then extend back up.
safetyNotes: Keep the upper arms still and do not flare the elbows wide.

exercises.name: Triceps Kickback
exercises.description: Bent-over dumbbell kickback that peaks tension at full elbow extension.
instructions: Hinge forward with a flat back and pin the upper arm parallel to the torso.
Extend the elbow to straighten the arm behind you, squeeze, then return.
safetyNotes: Keep the upper arm still — only the forearm moves.

exercises.name: Hack Squat (Machine)
exercises.description: Machine squat with the back supported on an angled sled to load the quads safely.
instructions: Set the shoulders and back against the pads with feet mid-platform.
Unlock and squat to at least parallel, then drive back up without locking out hard.
safetyNotes: Keep the lower back flat against the pad throughout.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat
exercises.description: Guided-bar squat on the Smith machine for a stable, fixed squat path.
instructions: Set the bar across the upper back and place the feet slightly forward.
Unlock and squat to depth, then drive up and re-rack at the top.
safetyNotes: Keep the core braced; the fixed bar path does not replace good control.

exercises.name: Sumo Squat
exercises.description: Wide-stance, toes-out squat biasing the inner thighs and glutes.
instructions: Take a wide stance with toes turned out, holding a dumbbell at the chest or between the legs.
Squat straight down keeping the knees tracking over the toes, then drive up.
safetyNotes: Keep the chest up and knees pushed out, not caving in.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up
exercises.description: Single-leg step onto a box for unilateral quad and glute strength and balance.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and place one foot fully on a knee-height box.
Drive through the top foot to stand tall, then lower under control and repeat.
safetyNotes: Push through the heel of the top foot; avoid pushing off the trailing foot.

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.description: Barbell hip hinge with the bar on the back to load the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Set the bar on the upper back and take a hip-width stance with soft knees.
Hinge at the hips pushing them back until the torso is near parallel, then stand tall.
safetyNotes: Use light loads and a neutral spine — this loads the lower back directly.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Anchored eccentric bodyweight hamstring curl — one of the hardest hamstring builders.
instructions: Kneel with the ankles anchored and the body straight from knees to head.
Lower forward as slowly as possible resisting with the hamstrings, then push up to assist back.
safetyNotes: Only lower as far as you can control; catch yourself with the hands.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.description: Seated machine hamstring curl flexing the knees against a pad.
instructions: Sit with the shin pad on the lower calves and the thigh pad locked down.
Curl the heels under the seat squeezing the hamstrings, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips down and avoid jerking the weight.

exercises.name: Cable Glute Kickback
exercises.description: Single-leg cable kickback driving the hip into extension to isolate the glute.
instructions: Attach an ankle strap to a low pulley and face the stack, hinged slightly forward.
Drive the working leg back squeezing the glute, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back to gain range — move from the hip.

exercises.name: Donkey Calf Raise
exercises.description: Hip-hinged calf raise that stretches the calves under load for a long range.
instructions: Hinge forward with the pad on the lower back and the balls of the feet on the platform.
Rise onto the toes squeezing the calves, then lower into a deep stretch.
safetyNotes: Control the bottom stretch; do not bounce out of it.

exercises.name: Leg Press Calf Raise
exercises.description: Calf raise performed on the leg-press platform for heavy loadable plantar flexion.
instructions: Place the balls of the feet on the bottom edge of the leg-press platform, legs nearly straight.
Press the platform away by extending the ankles, then lower into a stretch.
safetyNotes: Keep a slight knee bend and never fully lock the knees under load.

exercises.name: Sit-Up
exercises.description: Full-range trunk flexion from lying to sitting for the abdominals and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat or anchored.
Curl up to a seated position, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Avoid yanking on the neck — lead with the chest.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunch
exercises.description: Alternating elbow-to-knee crunch that targets the abs and obliques.
instructions: Lie on your back, hands by the ears, and lift the shoulders and feet off the floor.
Twist to bring each elbow to the opposite knee while extending the other leg, alternating.
safetyNotes: Rotate from the torso, not by pulling on the neck.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Raise
exercises.description: Floor leg raise lifting straight legs to target the lower abs.
instructions: Lie flat with the legs straight and hands tucked under the hips.
Raise the legs to vertical keeping them straight, then lower slowly without touching down.
safetyNotes: Press the lower back into the floor; do not let it arch.

exercises.name: Reverse Crunch
exercises.description: Hips-off-the-floor curl that rolls the pelvis up to bias the lower abs.
instructions: Lie on your back with the knees bent and lifted over the hips.
Curl the pelvis up bringing the knees toward the chest, then lower under control.
safetyNotes: Move with the abs, not by swinging the legs.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.description: Anti-extension rollout on an ab wheel that powerfully braces the entire core.
instructions: Kneel holding the ab wheel under the shoulders with a braced core.
Roll forward as far as you can control without arching, then pull back to the start.
safetyNotes: Keep the ribs down and the back flat — never let the lower back sag.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Isometric side hold that braces the obliques and lateral core.
instructions: Lie on your side propped on the forearm with the body in a straight line.
Lift the hips so only the forearm and feet touch, and hold for time.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips stacked and lifted; do not let them drop.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.description: Gymnastics isometric hold pressing the low back down with limbs extended.
instructions: Lie on your back, press the lower back into the floor and lift the shoulders and legs.
Extend the arms overhead and hold the dished position for time.
safetyNotes: If the lower back lifts, raise the legs higher to keep it pinned down.

exercises.name: Cable Woodchopper
exercises.description: Diagonal cable chop that trains rotational power through the obliques.
instructions: Set the pulley high and stand side-on, gripping the handle with both hands.
Pull and rotate down and across to the opposite hip, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Pivot the back foot and rotate from the trunk, not the arms.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.description: Anti-rotation cable press that teaches the core to resist twisting.
instructions: Stand side-on to a cable at chest height holding the handle at the sternum.
Press the handle straight out resisting the pull's rotation, hold, then return.
safetyNotes: Keep the hips and shoulders square — do not let the cable twist you.

exercises.name: Clean and Press
exercises.description: Full-body lift pulling the bar from the floor to the shoulders then overhead.
instructions: Pull the bar explosively from the floor to the shoulders, dropping under to catch it.
Brace and press or push the bar overhead to lockout, then return under control.
safetyNotes: Learn the clean technique with light loads before adding weight.

exercises.name: Thruster
exercises.description: Front squat flowing directly into an overhead press — a brutal full-body conditioner.
instructions: Hold the weight at the shoulders and squat to depth.
Drive up explosively and use the momentum to press the weight overhead, then return to the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Keep the core braced and the bar path vertical.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up
exercises.description: Slow, multi-step floor-to-standing lift under a single weight for total-body control.
instructions: Lie down pressing one weight up; rise to a seated, then kneeling, then standing position keeping the arm locked overhead.
Reverse each step under control back to lying.
safetyNotes: Keep eyes on the weight and the arm vertical throughout; start very light.

exercises.name: 3/4 Sit-Up
exercises.description: 3/4 Sit-Up is a beginner compound strength exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the abs.
instructions: Lie down on the floor and secure your feet. Your legs should be bent at the knees.
Place your hands behind or to the side of your head. You will begin with your back on the ground. This will be your starting position.
Flex your hips and spine to raise your torso toward your knees.
At the top of the contraction your torso should be perpendicular to the ground. Reverse the motion, going only ¾ of the way down.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hamstring
exercises.description: 90/90 Hamstring is a beginner compound mobility exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the hamstrings, and also engages the calves.
instructions: Lie on your back, with one leg extended straight out.
With the other leg, bend the hip and knee to 90 degrees. You may brace your leg with your hands if necessary. This will be your starting position.
Extend your leg straight into the air, pausing briefly at the top. Return the leg to the starting position.
Repeat for 10-20 repetitions, and then switch to the other leg.

exercises.name: Ab Crunch Machine
exercises.description: Ab Crunch Machine is an intermediate isolation strength exercise performed with a machine. It primarily works the abs.
instructions: Select a light resistance and sit down on the ab machine placing your feet under the pads provided and grabbing the top handles. Your arms should be bent at a 90 degree angle as you rest the triceps on the pads provided. This will be your starting position.

At the same time, begin to lift the legs up as you crunch your upper torso. Breathe out as you perform this movement. Tip: Be sure to use a slow and controlled motion. Concentrate on using your abs to move the weight while relaxing your legs and feet.
After a second pause, slowly return to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Ab Roller
exercises.description: Ab Roller is an intermediate compound strength exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the abs, and also engages the shoulders.
instructions: Hold the Ab Roller with both hands and kneel on the floor.
Now place the ab roller on the floor in front of you so that you are on all your hands and knees (as in a kneeling push up position). This will be your starting position.

Slowly roll the ab roller straight forward, stretching your body into a straight position. Tip: Go down as far as you can without touching the floor with your body. Breathe in during this portion of the movement.
After a pause at the stretched position, start pulling yourself back to the starting position as you breathe out. Tip: Go slowly and keep your abs tight at all times.

exercises.name: Adductor
exercises.description: Adductor is an intermediate isolation mobility exercise performed with a foam roll. It primarily works the adductors.
instructions: Lie face down with one leg on a foam roll.
Rotate the leg so that the foam roll contacts against your inner thigh. Shift as much weight onto the foam roll as can be tolerated.
While trying to relax the muscles if the inner thigh, roll over the foam between your hip and knee, holding points of tension for 10-30 seconds. Repeat with the other leg.

exercises.name: Adductor/Groin
exercises.description: Adductor/Groin is an intermediate compound mobility exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the adductors.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet raised towards the ceiling.
Have your partner hold your feet or ankles. Abduct your legs as far as you can. This will be your starting position.
Attempt to squeeze your legs together for 10 or more seconds, while your partner prevents you from doing so.

Now, relax the muscles in your legs as your partner pushes your feet apart, stretching as far as is comfortable for you. Be sure to let your partner know when the stretch is adequate to prevent overstretching or injury.

exercises.name: Advanced Kettlebell Windmill
exercises.description: Advanced Kettlebell Windmill is an intermediate isolation strength exercise performed with kettlebells. It primarily works the abs, and also engages the glutes, hamstrings and shoulders.
instructions: Clean and press a kettlebell overhead with one arm.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Keep the non-working arm behind your back and turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the kettlebell.
Lower yourself as far as possible.
Pause for a second and reverse the motion back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Air Bike
exercises.description: Air Bike is a beginner compound strength exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the abs.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with your lower back pressed to the ground. For this exercise, you will need to put your hands beside your head. Be careful however to not strain with the neck as you perform it. Now lift your shoulders into the crunch position.
Bring knees up to where they are perpendicular to the floor, with your lower legs parallel to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Now simultaneously, slowly go through a cycle pedal motion kicking forward with the right leg and bringing in the knee of the left leg. Bring your right elbow close to your left knee by crunching to the side, as you breathe out.
Go back to the initial position as you breathe in.
Crunch to the opposite side as you cycle your legs and bring closer your left elbow to your right knee and exhale.
Continue alternating in this manner until all of the recommended repetitions for each side have been completed.

exercises.name: All Fours Quad Stretch
exercises.description: All Fours Quad Stretch is an intermediate compound mobility exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the quadriceps, and also engages the quadriceps.
instructions: Start off on your hands and knees, then lift your leg off the floor and hold the foot with your hand.
Use your hand to hold the foot or ankle, keeping the knee fully flexed, stretching the quadriceps and hip flexors.
Focus on extending your hips, thrusting them towards the floor. Hold for 10-20 seconds and then switch sides.

exercises.name: Alternate Hammer Curl
exercises.description: Alternate Hammer Curl is a beginner isolation strength exercise performed with a dumbbell. It primarily works the biceps, and also engages the forearms.
instructions: Stand up with your torso upright and a dumbbell in each hand being held at arms length. The elbows should be close to the torso.
The palms of the hands should be facing your torso. This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm stationary, curl the right weight forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. Continue the movement until your biceps is fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze the biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Slowly begin to bring the dumbbells back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat the movement with the left hand. This equals one repetition.
Continue alternating in this manner for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Alternate Heel Touchers
exercises.description: Alternate Heel Touchers is a beginner isolation strength exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the abs.
instructions: Lie on the floor with the knees bent and the feet on the floor around 18-24 inches apart. Your arms should be extended by your side. This will be your starting position.
Crunch over your torso forward and up about 3-4 inches to the right side and touch your right heel as you hold the contraction for a second. Exhale while performing this movement.
Now go back slowly to the starting position as you inhale.

Now crunch over your torso forward and up around 3-4 inches to the left side and touch your left heel as you hold the contraction for a second. Exhale while performing this movement and then go back to the starting position as you inhale. Now that both heels have been touched, that is considered 1 repetition.
Continue alternating sides in this manner until all prescribed repetitions are done.

exercises.name: Alternate Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.description: Alternate Incline Dumbbell Curl is a beginner isolation strength exercise performed with a dumbbell. It primarily works the biceps, and also engages the forearms.
instructions: Sit down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand being held at arms length. Tip: Keep the elbows close to the torso.This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm stationary, curl the right weight forward while contracting the biceps as you breathe out. As you do so, rotate the hand so that the palm is facing up. Continue the movement until your biceps is fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze the biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Slowly begin to bring the dumbbell back to starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat the movement with the left hand. This equals one repetition.
Continue alternating in this manner for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Alternate Leg Diagonal Bound
exercises.description: Alternate Leg Diagonal Bound is a beginner compound strength exercise performed with bodyweight. It primarily works the quadriceps, and also engages the glutes, adductors, calves and hamstrings.
instructions: Assume a comfortable stance with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Begin by pushing off with the front leg, driving the opposite knee forward and as high as possible before landing. Attempt to cover as much distance to each side with each bound.
It may help to use a line on the ground to guage distance from side to side.
Repeat the sequence with the other leg.

exercises.name: Alternating Cable Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Alternating Cable Shoulder Press is a beginner compound strength exercise performed with a cable. It primarily works the shoulders, and also engages the triceps.
instructions: Move the cables to the bottom of the tower and select an appropriate weight.
Grasp the cables and hold them at shoulder height, palms facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your head and chest up, extend through the elbow to press one side directly over head.
After pausing at the top, return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Alternating Deltoid Raise
exercises.description: Alternating Deltoid Raise is a beginner isolation strength exercise performed with a dumbbell. It primarily works the shoulders.
instructions: In a standing position, hold a pair of dumbbells at your side.
Keeping your elbows slightly bent, raise the weights directly in front of you to shoulder height, avoiding any swinging or cheating.
Return the weights to your side.
On the next repetition, raise the weights laterally, raising them out to your side to about shoulder height.
Return the weights to the starting position and continue alternating to the front and side.

exercises.name: Alternating Floor Press
exercises.description: Alternating Floor Press is a beginner compound strength exercise performed with kettlebells. It primarily works the chest, and also engages the abs, shoulders and triceps.
instructions: Lie on the floor with two kettlebells next to your shoulders.
Position one in place on your chest and then the other, gripping the kettlebells on the handle with the palms facing forward.
Extend both arms, so that the kettlebells are being held above your chest. Lower one kettlebell, bringing it to your chest and turn the wrist in the direction of the locked out kettlebell.
Raise the kettlebell and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Alternating Hang Clean
exercises.description: Alternating Hang Clean is an intermediate compound strength exercise performed with kettlebells. It primarily works the hamstrings, and also engages the biceps, calves, forearms, glutes, lower back and traps.
instructions: Place two kettlebells between your feet. To get in the starting position, push your butt back and look straight ahead.

Clean one kettlebell to your shoulder and hold on to the other kettlebell in a hanging position. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrist as you do so.
Lower the cleaned kettlebell to a hanging position and clean the alternate kettlebell. Repeat.

exercises.name: Alternating Kettlebell Press
exercises.description: Alternating Kettlebell Press is an intermediate compound strength exercise performed with kettlebells. It primarily works the shoulders, and also engages the triceps.
instructions: Clean two kettlebells to your shoulders. Clean the kettlebells to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebells towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrists as you do so.
Press one directly overhead by extending through the elbow, turning it so the palm faces forward while holding the other kettlebell stationary .
Lower the pressed kettlebell to the starting position and immediately press with your other arm.

exercises.name: Alternating Kettlebell Row
exercises.description: Alternating Kettlebell Row is an intermediate isolation strength exercise performed with kettlebells. It primarily works the upper back, and also engages the biceps and upper back.
